A small black Ridge logo (diamond shape) centered at the very bottom. SPACING: Tweet has standard social media card padding (~16px). Generous whitespace around the wallets. MOOD: Trust-building through authentic user testimonial, clean and editorial, comparison visual (old bulky wallet vs sleek Ridge wallet) reinforces the product value.

LAYOUT: Landscape/wide rectangular ad. Full-bleed lifestyle photo as background showing a person walking on a wet city sidewalk — only lower legs and feet visible. Text overlay on the left side. HEADLINE: 'Where Comfort Meets Waterproof' in bold white sans-serif text, ~24px equivalent, left-aligned in the upper-left quadrant. SUBTEXT: 'GET OUTSIDE THIS WINTER' in smaller white uppercase tracking-wide sans-serif, ~10px, below the headline. LOGO: 'VESSI' in white uppercase bold sans-serif at the top-left corner. Overall palette is gray (#6B7280), charcoal (#374151), white (#FFFFFF). SPACING: Text is left-aligned with ~8% padding from left edge. Headline and subtext have ~8px gap between them. MOOD: Urban, rainy-day lifestyle, practical yet stylish. The wet environment visually demonstrates the waterproof claim. Clean, minimal, aspirational outdoor brand feel.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a very light sage/mint green (#F0F7F0) background. Products arranged in a centered cluster in the middle. The multiple benefit callouts create a 'reasons to believe' framework. Australian-made and cruelty-free badges build ethical credibility. Soft, gentle, approachable beauty brand.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a warm orange (#F97316) background that has a slight gradient/texture. Small decorative star/sparkle elements scattered in the upper area. Products positioned in the center-left area. Text in the lower portion. PRODUCT: An OLIPOP 'Orange Cream' sparkling tonic can and an orange creamsicle popsicle positioned side by side. The can is on the left, the popsicle on the right, both roughly centered. Together they span about 55% of the ad width. The can features the OLIPOP branding with orange and cream stripe design. Small white star/sparkle decorations. SPACING: Products centered with ~20% side margins. Headline sits in the lower 30% with ~5% bottom padding. Logo at top with ~5% top padding. MOOD: Fun, nostalgic, playful. The 'Guess who's back' is a pop-culture reference creating excitement about a returning flavor. LAYOUT: Square ad designed to look like a Google Search results page screenshot. The top portion mimics the Google search interface with the Google logo, search bar, and navigation tabs. Below that is a grid of image search results showing various product photos. PRODUCT: 'Yes You Can' alcohol-free spritz cans — multiple cans visible in the image grid. The most prominent is a large pink/coral can in the right side of the grid with 'YES YOU CAN' in bold white uppercase text. Other cans in yellow, dark/black varieties visible in the grid thumbnails on the left. HEADLINE: The Google search bar contains the query text: 'The best drink this summer!' in standard Google search font. This functions as the implicit headline/claim. SUBTEXT: Google navigation tabs ('All', 'Shopping', 'Images', 'Videos', 'News', 'Books') visible below the search bar, adding to the authenticity of the Google screenshot look. CTA: No explicit CTA — the format itself (appearing as a top Google result) is the social proof mechanism. COLORS: White (#FFFFFF) Google interface, light gray (#F1F3F4) search bar background, standard Google blue (#4285F4) for 'All' tab underline, pink/coral (#E85D75) prominent can, yellow and dark cans in grid. Light pink (#FDE8EC) overall background tint at the bottom. SPACING: Mimics exact Google search page spacing — search bar centered with standard padding, image grid with standard gap spacing. MOOD: Clever, authoritative social proof through implied search dominance. By framing the product as 'the best drink this summer' in a Google search context, it implies consensus/popularity. The fake screenshot format is attention-grabbing and thumb-stopping in social feeds.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a muted sage/forest green (#4A7C59) background. Center features a product pouch held by two hands from below. Four white review cards are arranged around the product — two in the upper corners, two in the lower corners — slightly overlapping the product, creating depth. Each card is a white rounded rectangle with: five yellow/gold star rating at the top, a quote in bold dark serif text, and a reviewer name in small colored text below. SUBTEXT: Review card contents — Top-left: 'Best Superfood blend ever.' (by ANTONY), Top-right: 'My go-to meal every morning.' (by KAREN), Bottom-left: 'I can't believe how cheap this is.' (by DANIEL B), Bottom-right: 'I can't believe they exist...' (by LAURA M). Each quote is in bold black serif ~11px text. Names in small purple/indigo text. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The four reviews covering different value propositions (quality, convenience, price, uniqueness) create comprehensive social proof. The hands holding the product add human warmth.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a vertical split background — left half is a light lavender/purple (#E8E0F0), right half is a slightly different shade of light blue-purple (#D8D0E8). A tall glass of iced coffee is centered, straddling both halves. Benefit callouts are arranged on both sides of the glass. PRODUCT: A tall clear glass of iced coffee with milk — showing layered coffee and cream, ice cubes visible. The glass is centered and spans roughly 30% of the ad width, taking up about 60% of the vertical height. HEADLINE: 'COFFEE meets PROTEIN' at the top — 'COFFEE' in bold uppercase dark purple/navy sans-serif on the left, 'meets' in a smaller light cursive/script purple, 'PROTEIN' in bold uppercase dark purple/navy sans-serif on the right. ~24px for the bold words, ~16px for 'meets'. SUBTEXT: Three benefit callouts on each side of the glass. Left side: '115mg caffeine', '100% arabica coffee', 'Smooth and delicious'. LAYOUT: Square ad with a vivid lime-yellow-green gradient background — transitioning from bright yellow-green on the left to neon green on the right (#BFFF00 to #7FFF00). Product centered. '50%' is noticeably larger/bolder than the surrounding text for emphasis. LAYOUT: Square ad designed to look like a news article or editorial piece. Top section has a white bar with publication branding. Below is a two-column image section showing product photos. PRODUCT: BREZ non-intoxicating microdose beverage — shown as a holographic/iridescent tall slim can held in a hand (left image), and a second can alongside a cocktail coupe glass (right image). LAYOUT: Square ad with a vertical 50/50 split — left half is hot pink (#FF1493), right half is light baby pink (#FFB6C1). Brand logo spans the top center straddling both halves. LAYOUT: Square ad with a warm peach/salmon (#F4A985) gradient background at the bottom, transitioning to a light cream/off-white (#FFF8F0) at the top. A large quote card dominates the upper portion. Product shown in the lower-right. A tropical leaf peeks in from the lower-left corner. Leaf as decorative element lower-left. MOOD: Clean, wellness-oriented, problem-solution testimonial. The quote identifies a relatable pain point (managing multiple vitamin bottles) and the product is positioned as the solution. Warm, approachable, health-conscious.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a light pink (#FDE8EC) background. Three horizontal product-benefit rows stacked vertically, each containing a product image on the left and descriptive text on the right. LAYOUT: Wide landscape/rectangular ad with a clean white/cream background. Left side features a person photo, right side has text. Bottom section has a row of media/press logos. LAYOUT: Small square ad designed to look like a Forbes magazine article screenshot. The ad is framed/tilted at a slight angle on a dark textured background, appearing as if it is a phone or tablet screenshot placed on a surface. Top has the Forbes masthead, followed by a headline, then an article body with an embedded product photo. PRODUCT: A beard grooming kit shown in the article photo — an open black box/case containing beard care tools and products, positioned in the lower portion of the 'article' screenshot. SUBTEXT: Article body text visible below the headline in smaller serif font, describing beard growth products and experience. The Forbes logo/masthead at the top provides the authority frame. CTA: No explicit CTA button — the Forbes editorial format provides implicit authority-based conversion pressure. Internal article layout follows standard editorial spacing. ~15% dark background margin visible around the screenshot. MOOD: High-authority press social proof. By framing the ad as a Forbes article, it borrows enormous credibility. The tilted/screenshot presentation makes it feel like someone is sharing a discovery ('look what I found in Forbes'). Masculine, aspirational, credible for a male grooming product.

LAYOUT: Square ad with a dark charcoal/near-black (#1A1A1A) background. Designed to mimic an incoming phone call screen. Product image centered in the middle portion. Text at top, phone call UI elements at the bottom. PRODUCT: A RYZE mushroom matcha powder packet — a kraft/brown paper pouch with a colorful illustrated label showing 'RYZE' branding and botanical/mushroom artwork. The pouch is centered, roughly 35% of ad width, placed within a lighter tan/kraft square frame that looks like a notification or photo. HEADLINE: 'Your Daily Scoop' in large bold decorative green (#7FFF00 lime green) serif/display font at the top, ~24px. SUBTEXT: 'Digestion Support Calling' in bold bright green sans-serif below the headline, ~16px. At the bottom, mimicking an iPhone incoming call UI: a green phone icon with 'Remind Me' label on the left, 'slide to answer' text in the center with a slide indicator, and a white message icon with 'Message' label on the right. CTA: The 'slide to answer' phone call UI metaphor acts as the CTA — implying the user should 'answer the call' by engaging with the product. The green phone button and white message button mimic iOS call screen exactly. COLORS: Dark charcoal/black (#1A1A1A) background, bright lime green (#7FFF00) headline text, lighter green for subtext, kraft/brown product packaging, white (#FFFFFF) for message icon and slide text, green (#22C55E) for phone icon. SPACING: Headline centered at top ~10% padding. Product centered vertically in a lighter framed area. Phone call UI elements at bottom ~10% from edge, spread evenly across width. MOOD: Clever, attention-grabbing, native-format social proof. By mimicking an incoming phone call, it creates an instinctive 'answer' response. The 'Digestion Support Calling' personifies the product benefit. Dark/moody aesthetic with bright green accents feels modern and tech-savvy. The phone call metaphor implies urgency — your health is 'calling.'

LAYOUT: Small square ad with a dark/black background split into two halves — left half is a close-up portrait photo of a smiling woman, right half is a dark panel with a customer testimonial quote. White (#FFFFFF) text on the dark panel. Green (#22C55E) in the quote text for emphasis on certain words. SPACING: 50/50 left-right split. Portrait fills the entire left half edge-to-edge. Quote text on the right has ~10% padding on all sides. Text is left-aligned within the right panel. MOOD: Authentic, personal, transformation-story social proof. They are centered, each occupying about 20% width, positioned in the middle 60% of the ad height. HEADLINE: 'SUPER SAVER COMBO' in large serif/decorative gold-toned text at the top center. 'SUPER SAVER' is larger, 'COMBO' slightly smaller below it. SUBTEXT: Product names appear near each bottle in small serif text. 'TURMERIC ROSEHIP MUKHALEYA' on the left, 'LAVENDER FLORA JAL' on the right with brief benefit descriptions. CTA: No explicit CTA button. Pricing shown on the right: original price (crossed out, ~876 in strikethrough) and sale price '399' in bold, both in dark/red tones. COLORS: Background is warm cream/beige (#F5E6D3) with a subtle aged paper texture. Text is dark brown/gold. Price uses red/coral for the sale price. Products have yellow/amber and white/green packaging. SPACING: Moderate spacing. Products have breathing room between them. MOOD: Ayurvedic, natural, premium yet affordable. The combo deal format emphasizes value. Warm earth tones convey natural/organic positioning.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Centered circular composition. Headline text at top spanning full width. A large thin circular ring frames a central photograph of a pregnant woman. Four problem callouts are placed around the circle in small oval/pill badges at the four quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right). PRODUCT: No physical product shown. The ad is for pregnancy pillows (the brand sells premium ones). The pregnant woman in the center is shown from chest to belly, wearing a light blue chambray shirt, with one hand raised in a 'stop' gesture and the other on her belly. She occupies about 60% of the ad height within the circle. HEADLINE: '4 REASONS Why you should avoid Cheap Pregnancy Pillows' at the top. The '4' is an enormous bold 3D-style number in coral/terracotta color (#CC6644), about 60px equivalent, positioned at the top-left. 'REASONS' is in large bold dark brown uppercase sans-serif next to it. 'Why you should avoid' is in regular weight cursive/script font below. 'Cheap' is in regular weight and 'Pregnancy Pillows' is in bold, both dark brown sans-serif. SUBTEXT: Four oval pill-shaped badges with coral/pink (#D4776B) backgrounds and white text positioned around the circular frame: 'Lack of Support' (bottom-left), 'Short Lifespan' (top-right), 'Dangerous Recycled Filling' (bottom-left), 'Produces More Heat' (bottom-right). Each has a small relevant icon. Text is ~10px white, with key words in bold. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a very soft peach/blush (#FDE8DC). Circle ring is coral/terracotta (#CC6644). Problem badges are coral pink (#D4776B) with white text. The large '4' has a 3D shadow effect in darker coral. Woman wears light blue. SPACING: The circular frame provides natural spacing. Badges are evenly distributed around the circle. Generous padding on all edges. MOOD: Educational, warning-style. Positions cheap competitors as dangerous/inferior. The 'stop' hand gesture reinforces the warning message. Feminine, warm color palette for pregnancy audience.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 16:9). Two-column layout with a strong diagonal split. Left column (~55%) has a red background with white headline text. Right column (~45%) has a white/light background with a phone mockup and dark text CTA. PRODUCT: A smartphone (shown at a slight tilt) displaying the Bradesco banking app interface with a red-themed UI showing loan/payment options. The phone occupies the right ~40% of the ad, centered vertically in the right column. HEADLINE: 'EMPRESTIMO HOJE. 1a PARCELA DAQUI A 3 MESES.' (Portuguese for 'Loan today. First installment in 3 months.') in bold white sans-serif uppercase text on the red background, left-aligned. '3 MESES' is the largest text element. Font ~18-24px equivalent. SUBTEXT: On the right side, in dark/black bold sans-serif text: 'ABRA SUA CONTA PELO APP.' ('Open your account through the app.'). The Bradesco logo (red tree icon) appears at the top right. CTA: The subtext itself functions as the CTA directing to the app. No separate button. COLORS: Left section is Bradesco brand red (#CC0000). Right section is white/light gray. Text on red is white; text on white is dark charcoal/black. Bradesco logo uses red. Phone screen shows red-themed app UI. SPACING: Clean split layout. Text is left-aligned on the red side with comfortable margins. Phone has natural whitespace around it. MOOD: Corporate, trustworthy banking. Problem (needing money now) with solution (loan with delayed payments). Bold red conveys urgency and brand recognition.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Side-by-side comparison layout split vertically down the middle. Left half features the brand product (Magic Spoon), right half features a generic competitor ('Others'). Top section shows product photos with brand names/labels. Bottom section shows a 3-row nutritional comparison grid with alternating row backgrounds. PRODUCT: Left side: a white bowl of colorful ring-shaped cereal (like Froot Loops style) with a black spoon, shot from above, surrounded by a purple/violet circular highlight ring. Right side: a muted pink/mauve bowl of plain brown/chocolate cereal puffs, shown smaller and less appealing. HEADLINE: 'MAGIC SPOON' logo in stylized purple text with a registered trademark symbol at top-left. 'Others' in plain gray serif text at top-right. No traditional headline - the comparison format is the hook. SUBTEXT: Three comparison rows spanning full width, each with the Magic Spoon value on the left (in bold purple) and competitor value on the right (in regular gray/dark text): Row 1: '0g' vs '12g' for 'SUGAR' (center label). Row 2: '4-5g' vs '30g' for 'NET CARBS'. Row 3: '12-14g' vs '1g' for 'PROTEIN'. The center column labels (SUGAR, NET CARBS, PROTEIN) are in uppercase gray sans-serif. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The stark nutritional contrast serves as the persuasion mechanism. COLORS: Background is a soft gradient from light blue (#E0F0FF) on the left to light pink/lavender (#F0E0F0) on the right, creating a subtle brand vs. generic visual distinction. Magic Spoon values are in deep purple (#6B21A8). Competitor values are in dark gray. The cereal bowl highlight ring is purple. Comparison row backgrounds alternate between very light purple/white. SPACING: Clean grid layout. Rows are evenly spaced with ~15px padding. Product photos have equal vertical space. Generous margins on all sides. MOOD: Health-conscious, comparative, confident. The visual contrast (colorful vs. plain, purple vs. gray) makes the brand look clearly superior. Data-driven persuasion.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 16:9 or 2:1). Full-bleed dark photograph of a woman's lower body (hips/thighs) wearing dark leggings, shot from behind. Text overlay on the right side. Brand logo watermark at bottom-left corner. PRODUCT: Dark charcoal/black Merino wool leggings shown being worn. The leggings cover the entire background as a lifestyle product shot. A hand is placed on the hip, showing the fit and fabric texture. The phone pocket detail is visible on the thigh. HEADLINE: 'SAY HELLO TO' in small uppercase light gray/white text at top-right, followed by 'Piper & Stella' in large white serif/mixed font below it. 'Piper & Stella' is the brand/product line name, ~24px equivalent. SUBTEXT: 'Our Duralife Merino Wool Leggings in Two Different Styles!' in smaller white sans-serif text below the brand name, ~11px. A small white brand logo appears at bottom left. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a dark moody photograph - deep charcoal/dark gray tones of the leggings and skin. Text is white/light gray for contrast against the dark background. SPACING: Text is right-aligned in the right ~40% of the ad. Comfortable padding from edges. MOOD: Premium, lifestyle, understated luxury. The dark photography and minimal text create an elevated fashion feel. Product introduction format.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Centered product showcase with headline at top and benefit badges arranged in a circular pattern around the products. Three product bottles lined up horizontally in the center. Six small icon badges are arranged around the products at roughly the 10, 2, 4, 8 o'clock positions and two at the sides. PRODUCT: Three Eva Naturals serum bottles arranged in a row at center: a dark amber bottle of 'Skin Restoring Retinol Serum' (left), a dark amber bottle of 'Vitamin C+ Serum' (center), and a dark amber bottle of 'Collagen Peptide Complex Serum' (right). Each has a different colored label accent (green, pink/coral, blue). They occupy roughly 60% width and 35% height of the ad. HEADLINE: 'EVA NATURALS' BEST SELLERS:' in bold dark sans-serif at top, with 'eva naturals' script logo above. Below: 'The Secret to Beautiful Radiant Skin!' in a slightly smaller bold serif/sans-serif mix. ~18px equivalent. SUBTEXT: Six circular icon badges with labels around the products: 'Cruelty Free' (bunny icon), 'Mercury Free' (Hg icon), 'Animal Oil Free' (leaf icon), 'DEA/MEA/TEA Free', 'Paraben Free' (chemistry icon), 'Polyethylene Glycol Free'. Each badge is a small white icon with dark text label. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a teal/dark cyan gradient (#1A7A7A to #2D9999) creating a rich, natural-health feel. Headline text is white. Product bottles are dark amber/brown. Badge icons are white/light on the teal background. Label accents on bottles are green, pink, and blue. SPACING: Products are evenly spaced in the center. Badges are symmetrically arranged around the products with ~20px gaps. Comfortable top/bottom margins. MOOD: Clean, natural, trustworthy. The certification badges emphasize safety and purity. Teal color conveys health and nature. Premium skincare positioning.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Clean vertical split down the middle - left half is light blue (#D6E8F5), right half is light pink (#F5D6E0). Left side lists problems, right side shows the solution product. Brand logo at bottom-right corner. PRODUCT: A large white cylindrical supplement container labeled 'Obvi Collagenic BURN ELITE' with pink/magenta branding. The container occupies the right half of the ad, centered, taking up about 70% of the right column's height. Lid is white/silver. HEADLINE: 'PROBLEMS' in large bold black uppercase sans-serif on the left side at top (~24px). 'SOLUTION' in large bold black uppercase sans-serif on the right side at top (~24px). This creates the classic problem-solution framework. SUBTEXT: Three problem items listed vertically on the left side, each with a small black icon: 'PCOS BELLY' (with a belly/body icon), 'HORMONE IMBALANCES' (with a female body icon), 'LOW ENERGY' (with a person-at-desk icon). Text is bold black uppercase sans-serif, ~14px. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The product on the solution side is the implied CTA. 'obvi' brand logo in small pink text at bottom-right. COLORS: Left half: soft blue (#D6E8F5). Right half: soft pink-to-cream gradient (#F5D6E0 to #FFF0E8). Problem text is black. Product label uses hot pink/magenta (#E91E8C) on white. Icons are black. SPACING: Even vertical distribution of problem items on the left with ~30px spacing. Product is vertically centered on the right. Clean center dividing line. Generous margins. MOOD: Health-focused, feminine, empathetic. Acknowledges real health struggles (PCOS, hormones) then presents a simple solution. The blue-to-pink transition feels calming and supportive.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Vertical split down the middle - left half has a light blue (#D6E8F5) background, right half has a golden yellow (#F5C518) background. Each side has a brand name at top, product image in the middle, and three benefit/feature statements at the bottom. A white circular 'VS' badge sits at the center dividing line between the two products. PRODUCT: Left side: a cylindrical cream/white cardstock container with elephant illustrations and the 'first day' branding, with two gummy vitamins (red/orange) sitting in front. Right side: a plain generic white/gray supplement bottle with gray cap, representing generic kids vitamins. Both products are centered in their columns, ~30% height each. HEADLINE: 'first day' in large lowercase dark serif font on the left side at top. 'Most Kids Vitamins' in bold dark sans-serif on the right side at top. The 'VS' in a white circle (bold, ~16px) sits at the center overlap of the two products. SUBTEXT: Three rounded-pill statement badges on each side below the products. Left (first day): 'Made with 80% less sugar', 'Made to fill in the gaps in a kid\'s diet', 'Backed by modern science' - white pills with dark text. Right (competitors): 'Sugar free or full of sugar', 'Made with 100% or more of daily nutritional value', 'Made with outdated formulas' - white pills with dark text. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The comparison format drives the persuasion. COLORS: Left column: soft light blue (#D6E8F5). Right column: golden yellow (#F5C518). Text is dark charcoal/black throughout. Statement pills are white with rounded corners. VS circle is white with dark text. The first day packaging is warm cream/white with colorful accents. SPACING: Symmetric layout - both columns mirror each other in structure. Product images are vertically centered. Statement pills have ~10px vertical spacing. Even margins throughout. MOOD: Parental, educational, trust-building. The VS comparison clearly positions the brand as the thoughtful, modern choice vs. generic alternatives. Warm, child-friendly colors.

LAYOUT: Small square format (1:1). Full-bleed product photography background showing multiple products arranged on a red/coral surface with green leaf decorations. A large centered semi-transparent white circle overlays the center with text inside. A small CTA button sits at the bottom of the circle. PRODUCT: Multiple bath/body products (bottles, jars) from what appears to be an organic/natural brand are scattered around the frame on a red surface with fresh green leaves/herbs. Products have green, amber, brown, and white packaging. They frame the central text circle. HEADLINE: Inside the white circle overlay: 'Time for a refill? Subscribe & save 15%!' in dark serif/sans-serif text, centered. 'Time for a refill?' is on the first line, 'Subscribe & save 15%!' on the second. ~14px equivalent. CTA: 'Save Now!' in a small dark green or black rounded pill button at the bottom of the white circle. White text on dark background, ~30% of circle width. COLORS: Background surface is red/coral (#CC3333). Products have various natural-tone packaging (green, amber, brown). Decorative leaves are bright green. Central overlay is white with ~85% opacity. Text is dark charcoal/black. CTA button is dark green/black. SPACING: Products frame the edges. Central circle occupies about 50% of the ad. Text is centered within the circle with ~15px padding. MOOD: Natural, organic, subscription-focused. The refill messaging implies sustainability and repeat purchase. Festive red background with green accents creates an inviting, seasonal feel.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1) designed to look like a magazine clipping or editorial feature on a light gray background. A white slightly-rotated card/clipping sits centered on the gray background with a drop shadow. The card contains: publication masthead at top, product photo in the middle, article headline below, and a pull quote at the bottom. PRODUCT: A single Glossier lipstick (Generation G or similar) in a pink shade, shown uncapped at an angle with its pink tube/case beside it. The lipstick sits on a light surface with soft shadows. It occupies the center ~60% of the card. HEADLINE: 'BYRDIE' in large bold black uppercase serif/editorial masthead font at the top of the card (~28px), centered. Below the product photo: 'These Are the Best Beauty Products Under $25' in bold black serif font, centered, ~16px. SUBTEXT: A pull quote below the headline in smaller italic serif text, ~10px, gray/dark: a brief editorial quote about the product. 'By Anna Lewski' attribution in small text below. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The editorial/press format serves as social proof and implied endorsement. COLORS: Outer background is medium gray (#B0B0B0). Card is white (#FFFFFF) with a subtle drop shadow. Text is black. Lipstick is pink/coral. The card has a very slight rotation (1-2 degrees) for a casual, clipped-out feel. SPACING: The card has ~20px internal padding. Product photo has generous whitespace around it. The overall ad has ~30px margins around the card. MOOD: Editorial, press-worthy, aspirational. The magazine clipping format leverages third-party credibility. Minimal and elegant, feels like earned media rather than paid advertising.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Two-section vertical layout. Top ~60% is a lifestyle photo of a man (shirtless, smiling, using the product). Bottom ~40% is a white card section with a social media-style testimonial (username, verified badge, review text). A small product image is inset in the top-right of the photo. PRODUCT: A Manscaped Lawn Mower 5.0 body groomer/trimmer shown in two ways: 1) being held/used by the man in the lifestyle photo (partially visible), and 2) as a small product shot inset in the upper-right corner showing the trimmer and its dark packaging/box (~15% of ad width). The product is black/dark with metallic accents. HEADLINE: No traditional headline. The social media handle '@darnell.davis' with a blue verified checkmark serves as the attribution header for the testimonial. SUBTEXT: The testimonial review text in the bottom white section: 'Great product. Light weight and performs beautifully. My trust was in the air from other products with cutting but the lawn mower 5.0 really left me wanting to use it again.' in regular black sans-serif text, ~12px. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The authentic testimonial format is the persuasion mechanism. COLORS: Top photo has warm skin tones and a bathroom/mirror setting with neutral beige/warm lighting. Bottom section is clean white (#FFFFFF) with black text. Verified badge is blue (#1DA1F2 Twitter-style). Product packaging is dark purple/black with gold accents. SPACING: Photo fills the top section edge-to-edge. Testimonial card has ~15px padding. Username is left-aligned. Text is left-aligned with comfortable line height. MOOD: Authentic, masculine, UGC-style. The real person + verified review format builds trust. Casual, personal tone addresses grooming insecurity with a confident, positive review.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Three horizontal bands/sections. Top band (~25%): black background with bold headline text. Middle band (~15%): yellow background with subheadline text. Bottom band (~60%): split into two halves showing a side-by-side food comparison. PRODUCT: A bowl of Immi Ramen shown on the left side of the bottom section - a dark bowl with ramen noodles, egg, and toppings shot from above. On the right side, a Cobb Salad in a white plate for comparison. Each food item occupies ~50% of the bottom section width. HEADLINE: 'BODY by RAMEN.' in the top black band. 'BODY' is in large bold white sans-serif (~30px). 'by' is in smaller yellow italic/script text. 'RAMEN.' is in large bold yellow sans-serif (~30px). The period adds emphasis. SUBTEXT: In the yellow middle band: 'BECAUSE OUR RAMEN TASTES BETTER WITH 1/2 THE CALORIES.' in bold black uppercase sans-serif, centered, ~12px. Below in the bottom comparison section: 'IMMI RAMEN' in bold black text over the ramen image with '300 CALORIES' in very large bold black text (~28px). 'Cobb Salad' in regular gray text over the salad with '600 Calories' in large gray text (~28px). CTA: No explicit CTA button. The calorie comparison is the persuasion. COLORS: Top band is black (#000000) with white and yellow (#FFD700) text. Middle band is yellow (#FFD700) with black text. Bottom-left has a dark/moody food photo background. Bottom-right has a light/white background. The calorie numbers create visual contrast (black bold vs. gray regular). SPACING: Tight horizontal bands with no gaps. Food photos fill their sections edge-to-edge. Text is centered within each band. MOOD: Bold, confrontational, playful. Challenges the assumption that ramen is unhealthy by comparing it to a 'healthy' salad. The contrast between the indulgent ramen visual and the calorie number creates surprise.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Gradient background with a centered headline question at the top, two side-by-side comparison cards in the middle, and a full-width CTA button at the bottom. PRODUCT: No single product shown. Two food photographs are presented in rounded-corner white cards for comparison: Left card shows a colorful healthy bowl (avocado, turkey, lettuce wrap with various vegetables) shot from above. Right card shows a take-out burger with fries. Each card occupies ~45% width with a small gap between them. HEADLINE: 'WHY YOU\'RE NOT LOSING WEIGHT?' in bold dark sans-serif text at the top center, ~18px. 'LOSING WEIGHT' is in a larger, bolder, colored (teal/green) weight. The question mark makes it a hook. SUBTEXT: Each comparison card has: a price indicator ($ vs $$), star rating (4.5 stars vs 3 stars), a label pill ('balanced' in green vs 'less balanced' in orange/coral), and a bullet-point list of nutritional characteristics. Left card: 'Avocado & turkey lettuce wrap' with benefits like 'Low in calories & carbohydrates', 'High in vitamins and minerals', etc. in small green bullet points. Right card: 'Take-out burger' with downsides like 'High in calories, fat & sodium', 'May contribute to weight gain', etc. in small coral/red bullet points. Text is ~9-10px. CTA: 'TAKE THE TEST' in bold white uppercase text inside a large rounded-pill gradient button (purple-to-magenta gradient, #8B5CF6 to #EC4899), centered at the bottom, spanning ~50% width. COLORS: Background is a soft gradient from light green/mint (#E0F5E8) at top-left to light purple/lavender (#F0E0F5) at bottom-right. Cards are white with subtle shadows. Left card accents are green/teal (#10B981). Right card accents are coral/orange (#F97316). CTA button is a purple-to-pink gradient. Headline uses dark charcoal and teal. SPACING: Cards have ~15px internal padding. ~10px gap between cards. Headline has ~20px top margin. CTA has ~20px bottom margin. Bullet points have ~8px spacing. MOOD: Health-conscious, educational, quiz-funnel style. The comparison card format makes the healthy choice feel obvious. The 'Take the Test' CTA drives engagement through personalization. Soft gradients feel modern and wellness-oriented.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Clean white background. Centered vertical stack: headline at top (~20%), product arrangement in the center (~55%), CTA button below products (~10%), and disclaimer text at the very bottom (~5%). A small curved arrow illustration points from the headline toward the product. PRODUCT: An AG1 (Athletic Greens) welcome kit arrangement showing multiple items: a large tan/kraft cardboard box with 'AG1' branding (centered, largest element), a dark green canister of AG1 powder (right), a green pouch/bag of AG1 (right of box), AG1 travel packets (left-front), a glass bottle/shaker with green liquid (far left), and a small green measuring scoop (center-front). The entire product spread occupies about 60% width and 40% height, centered. HEADLINE: 'Get your free AG1 Welcome Kit*' in dark charcoal/black sans-serif text, centered at the top. 'Get your free' is in regular weight (~20px). 'AG1' has a small registered trademark. 'Welcome Kit' is in the same weight. The asterisk references terms at the bottom. SUBTEXT: '*Offer valid for new subscribers only' in small dark gray text at the very bottom center, ~9px. CTA: 'Claim Offer' in bold white text inside a large rounded-pill dark green button (#1B5E3B / forest green), centered below the product arrangement, spanning ~40% width. The button has generous padding making it prominent. COLORS: Background is clean white (#FFFFFF). Text is dark charcoal (#1A1A1A). CTA button is deep forest green (#1B5E3B) with white text. Product packaging uses various greens (forest green canister, sage green pouch, kraft cardboard). The curved arrow is dark teal/green. AG1 branding consistently uses green. SPACING: Generous whitespace on all sides (~25px margins). Headline has ~30px top padding. Products are carefully arranged with natural overlapping. CTA has ~15px gap from products. Disclaimer has ~10px bottom padding. MOOD: Premium, health-focused, generous. The 'free welcome kit' offer feels abundant. Clean white background with green accents conveys freshness and health. The product spread looks valuable and aspirational. Minimal design lets the offer speak for itself.

LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). Full-bleed dark navy/deep purple gradient background. Bold headline at top spanning full width. Left side has a vertical stack of 5 ingredient callouts, each with a small circular ingredient photo to its left and two lines of text (ingredient name in bold, benefit in lighter weight) to its right. Right-center area has a product image (metallic pouch/bag) angled slightly, taking up roughly 40% of the composition. A red rounded-rectangle CTA button sits at the bottom-right corner. PRODUCT: A dark branded pouch/packet (RYZE mushroom coffee) shown at a slight 3D angle, positioned center-right, occupying about 35-40% of the ad height. The pouch has a dark navy color with subtle branding. HEADLINE: 'NO MORE SLEEPLESS NIGHTS' in bold, large, all-caps white sans-serif font at the top of the ad, taking about 20% of the vertical space. Very high font weight (800+), tight letter-spacing. SUBTEXT: Five ingredient callouts stacked vertically on the left side: 'Reishi - Sweet Sleep', 'Cacao - Improved Blood Flow', 'Chicory - Digestive Support', 'Melatonin - Rest & Recovery', 'L-Theanine - Cognitive Function', 'Glycine - Deep Sleep'. Each ingredient name is in bold white, benefit text in lighter white/gray, smaller font. Small circular photos (~30px) of each ingredient to the left of each callout. CTA: 'TRY IT' in white text on a red/coral rounded-rectangle button, positioned at bottom-right corner. Button is approximately 80px wide by 35px tall with rounded corners (~8px radius). COLORS: Background is a deep navy-to-purple gradient (#1a1a3e to #2d1b4e). Text is white (#FFFFFF). CTA button is coral-red (#E84545). Ingredient photos have warm natural tones. SPACING: Generous padding (~20px) on all sides. Ingredient callouts have ~15px vertical spacing between them. MOOD: Calming, nighttime, wellness-focused. Premium health supplement feel with dark luxurious background suggesting nighttime use.

LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). Left half is a lifestyle photo of a smiling bearded man in a blue button-down shirt, outdoors with autumn/blurred background. Right half is a white/light background with text content. The headline question spans the full width at the top over both halves. Below on the right side, 4 benefit bullet points are stacked vertically, each with bold key metric and regular-weight comparison text. A bold stat line sits at the very bottom. PRODUCT: No product packaging shown directly; this is a meal/nutrition service ad (Huel-style). The man holding the product is the visual. HEADLINE: 'Trying to eat healthier this year?' in a large dark serif or rounded sans-serif font, positioned at the top spanning the full width. Text is dark gray/black, approximately 22-26px equivalent, medium-bold weight. Has a casual, question-based conversational tone. SUBTEXT: Four benefit lines stacked on the right half, each starting with 'More' in bold: 'More protein than 5 eggs', 'More potassium than a banana', 'More omega-3 than two tins of sardines', 'More vitamin C than a small orange'. Each line has the key nutrient word in bold black and the comparison in regular weight gray. At the bottom: '34 meals for $2.50 per meal' in bold black, acting as the value proposition/CTA. CTA: The bottom text '34 meals for $2.50 per meal' serves as the CTA in bold dark text, no button shape. COLORS: Left half has warm autumn photography tones (oranges, greens, warm light). Right half is clean white (#FFFFFF) or very light gray (#F8F8F8). Text is dark charcoal (#2D2D2D) with bold portions in near-black (#1A1A1A). SPACING: Clean separation between photo and text area. Bullet points have ~12-15px vertical spacing. Comfortable padding (~20px) around text area. MOOD: Healthy, approachable, relatable. The smiling man creates trust. Clean minimalist right side contrasts with warm lifestyle photo. Informational and comparison-driven.

LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). White background at top and bottom, with a large overhead product photo (matcha in a cup) centered in the middle taking up about 50% of the ad. Bold headline at the very top, centered. Subtitle text below the headline. Six ingredient callouts arranged in two columns (3 per side) flanking the central cup image, each with a small circular ingredient photo and two lines of text. Brand logo at bottom-right corner. Green accent color on the left and right edges (partial green shapes/leaves framing the cup). PRODUCT: A top-down view of a matcha latte in a white cup, showing vibrant green matcha with foamy texture. The cup is centered and is the dominant visual element, roughly 45% of the frame. HEADLINE: 'ENERGY WITHOUT THE JITTERS' in bold, large, all-caps black sans-serif font centered at the top. Very heavy weight (800-900), approximately 28-32px equivalent. SUBTEXT: Below headline: 'Our organic matcha ceremonial blend, enriched with 6 functional mushrooms:' in regular weight, smaller dark gray text, centered. Six ingredient callouts in two columns: Left column - 'LION'S MANE for focus', 'REISHI for stress', 'TURKEY TAIL for gut health'. Right column - 'SHIITAKE for immunity', 'CORDYCEPS for stamina', 'KING TRUMPET for inflammation'. Each has a small circular photo (~35px) of the mushroom and the name in bold with benefit in lighter weight below. CTA: No explicit CTA button. Brand logo 'RYZE' at bottom-right serves as brand anchor. COLORS: Background is white (#FFFFFF). Headline text is black (#1A1A1A). Accent green from matcha and decorative elements (#5BA84B or similar bright green). Ingredient callout backgrounds are small white circles with subtle shadows. SPACING: Symmetric layout with equal spacing on both sides. Ingredient callouts spaced ~20px apart vertically. ~15px padding around edges. MOOD: Fresh, energetic, health-conscious. Clean and organized, educational layout. The vibrant green matcha is the visual anchor. Natural and organic feel.

LAYOUT: Vertical/portrait ad (approximately 4:5 ratio). Very light gray (#F5F5F5) background throughout. Brand logo at top-left corner. Large bold headline below the logo, left-aligned, taking about 20% of vertical space. Below headline, a numbered list of 5 steps in a slightly inset white card area. Bottom third shows a grid of 12 book covers arranged in 2 rows of 6, each with a 'DAY X' label above it. PRODUCT: Headway app (book summary service). No physical product; the 12 colorful book covers at the bottom serve as the product showcase, displayed in a neat 6x2 grid. Each book cover is small (~50x70px equivalent) with vibrant colors. HEADLINE: 'How to cope with a loss of a Loved One' in large, bold, black serif font, left-aligned. Approximately 28-32px equivalent. Mixed case with key words capitalized. SUBTEXT: A numbered list of 5 steps: '1. Stop scrolling.', '2. Take a few deep breaths.', '3. Download Headway.' (Headway in green), '4. Read books that will help you with your loss.', '5. Start living again!' - Key words in each line are bolded. Text is dark gray, regular weight, ~14-16px. The list appears on a white or very slightly raised card. CTA: No explicit CTA button. 'Download Headway' in step 3 is colored green (#4CAF50) as an inline text CTA. COLORS: Background is light gray (#F2F2F2). Text is near-black (#222222). Headway brand green (#4CAF50) used for the logo icon and 'Headway' text in step 3. Book covers provide pops of blue, yellow, pink, orange, and teal. Day labels have subtle backgrounds. SPACING: Generous top padding (~25px). List items have ~10px vertical spacing. Book grid has ~8px gaps between covers. Overall very airy and clean. MOOD: Empathetic, calming, supportive. The content addresses grief in a gentle, actionable way. Clean minimalist design creates a sense of peace and order. The colorful books at the bottom add warmth and hope.

LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). Warm orange (#E8884D) solid background. Massive bold headline text at the top, partially obscured by the product, creating a layered depth effect. Product (sunscreen spray bottle) positioned on the left side, angled, sitting on/emerging from a diamond/rhombus-shaped orange cutout element. A white rounded-rectangle features card on the right side lists 4 benefits with checkmark icons. Dark CTA bar at the bottom-right. PRODUCT: A bright orange sunscreen spray bottle (SPF 50) positioned on the left side at a slight angle, taking up about 40% of the ad width and 60% of the height. HEADLINE: 'ULTIMATE SUNSCREEN' in extremely bold, massive all-caps white sans-serif font with dark outlines/shadows, positioned at the top. SUBTEXT: A white rounded-rectangle card on the right side with 'FEATURES' header in a dark bar at the top, then 4 benefit lines each with a purple/pink checkmark icon: 'PREVENTS PREMATURE AGING', 'PROTECTS AGAINST SKIN CANCER', 'IMPROVES SKIN TEXTURE', 'HELPS PREVENT ACNE'. Text is dark gray, all caps, ~11px, medium weight. CTA: 'GET IT NOW!' in white bold text on a dark charcoal/near-black (#333333) rounded-rectangle button at the bottom-right of the features card. COLORS: Background is warm orange (#E8884D). Product is bright orange (#F5923A). Headline text is white with dark shadow. Features card is white (#FFFFFF) with dark header bar. Checkmarks are purple/magenta (#9C27B0). CTA button is dark charcoal (#333333). SPACING: Product overlaps headline creating depth. Features card has ~10px padding internally, ~8px between benefit lines. MOOD: Bold, energetic, summer vibes. The orange dominance creates warmth and sun association. The overlapping headline-product technique adds visual dynamism. Direct and benefit-focused.

LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). Light beige/cream background (#F5EDE4). Product tube positioned on the left side, angled slightly, taking up about 40% of the width. Right side has large bold headline text and three benefit descriptions stacked vertically, each preceded by an orange horizontal line accent. Brand name 'Curology' at top-left corner in small text. PRODUCT: A dark navy blue squeeze tube (Curology Acne Cleanser) positioned on the left, angled at about 15 degrees. The tube has white text on dark blue packaging. Small orange accent on the tube as well. SPACING: Product on left with ~15px padding from edge. Text on right with ~15px right padding. Benefit descriptions have ~18-20px vertical spacing. LAYOUT: Square ad (1:1 ratio). Light warm beige/cream background. A 2x2 grid of lifestyle photos takes up the central ~70% of the ad. Brand name centered at the top above the grid. Same style as the rotated text. Brand name 'MAUVAIS' at top-center in a refined, spaced-out sans-serif font, lighter weight (~14px). CTA: No explicit CTA button. Thin white borders between photos. SPACING: ~15px padding on all sides. Photo grid gaps are ~4px. Text labels positioned right against the grid edges. Overall balanced and editorial. MOOD: Aspirational, summer lifestyle, editorial fashion. The warm-toned photos with cars and palms evoke luxury summer living. Clean grid layout feels magazine-like. Masculine and stylish.

SUBTEXT: Sender attribution: 'Kristy xx' with a small circular avatar photo next to it, positioned below the message bubble. Detox + Debloat relieves fullness + reduces abdominal bloating, based on traditional Western herbal medicine.' CTA: No explicit CTA button. LAYOUT: Horizontal/rectangular ad (approximately 3:1 or 4:1 wide ratio, like a social media comment/review screenshot). White background.
